title: Infosys Foundation USA awards $250,000 grant to NACCE for Digital Workforce Innovation Program
detailed_summary: Infosys Foundation USA awarded a $250,000 grant to NACCE for the Digital Workforce Innovation Program, in collaboration with MakerUSA. Six higher education institutions were selected to receive funding and support over the next 18 months. The program aims to reach over 3,000 students throughout two years and uses Infosys Springboard as a digital learning platform.

key_facts.fact: Infosys Foundation USA awarded a $250,000 grant to NACCE to advance the Digital Workforce Innovation Program.
key_facts.section_heading: Infosys Foundation USA Awards $250,000 Grant to NACCE to Advance Digital Workforce Innovation Program

key_facts.fact: Six higher education institutions were selected for the program: Coppin State University, Foothill College, GateWay Community College, Houston City College, Ivy Tech Community College, and North Iowa Area Community College.
The program aims to reach over 3,000 students throughout two years.
key_facts.section_heading: Infosys Foundation USA Awards $250,000 Grant to NACCE to Advance Digital Workforce Innovation Program

esg_or_csr_items.item: Infosys Foundation USA, NACCE and MakerUSA collaborated on the Digital Workforce Innovation Program to build digital skills and inclusive industry pathways.
